Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 3, 091 [Šumma Izbu 05][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[... UR].⸢MAḪ⸣ U₃.TU [...]-⸢MEŠ⸣ ZI-MEŠ LUGAL ⸢GABA⸣.[RI ...]

(o 1) [...] gives birth to a lion [...] they will arise; the king [...] opponent.

o 22

[... UR].⸢MAḪ⸣ U₃.TU-ma SIG₂-su x [... ma]-⸢li⸣-i KUR ma-la-a [x] x [x]

(o 2) [...] gives birth to a lion and it is covered ... [...] hair: the country ... [...] abundance.

o 33

[BE] U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma ma-li-⸢i⸣ [(...) ma]-⸢li⸣-i KUR ma-la-a x [x]

(o 3) [If] a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is covered in matted hair: the land will ... [...] abundance.

o 44

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SI-MEŠ ša₂ ⸢UZU⸣ [...] GABA.RI NU TUKUši

(o 4) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and horns of flesh [...]: [...] will not acquire an opponent.

o 55

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma IGI-šu₂ iš-ten ina ⸢SAG?⸣.[KI ...] LUGAL ŠU₂ ( o ) DABbat( o )

(o 5) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and (it has) one eye on the forehead: a king will seize the world.

o 66

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma KA-šu₂ up-pu-[uq] NIN BA.UŠ₂

(o 6) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its mouth is solid: a lady will die.

o 77

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma EME-šu₂ na-ḫi-ra-šu₂ [ik]-tum KA KUR LUGAL BALat

(o 7) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its tongue covers its nostrils: the opinion of the land will turn against the king.

o 88

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma EME-šu₂ IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ ik-tum LUGAL KUR-su BAL-su

(o 8) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its tongue covers its eyes: as for the king, its land will revolt against him.

o 99

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma 2 EME-ME-šu₂ INIM LUGAL UGU KUR DUGUDit LUGAL a-a-bi-šu₂ 1 MU KI.MIN KUR EN MAN-ma TUKU-ma

(o 9) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it has 2 tongues: the king's command will become important over the land; the king will ditto his enemies (in) 1 year; the land will acquire another lord and it will follow the strong one.

o 1010

( o o o o o o ) EGIR EN e-mu-qu ( o ) DUak

o 1111

[BE U₈] UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SAG.DU-su la₃ GAL₂ mu-ut ( o o o o o ) NUN

(o 11) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its head does not exist: death of a prince.

o 1212

[BE U₈ UR].MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SAG.DU MUŠ GARin <UR₅>.UŠ₂-MEŠ LUGAL—GI.NA ša₂ KUR i-be-lu

(o 12) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a snake's head: omen of Sargon, who ruled over the land.

o 1313

[BE U₈] ⸢UR⸣.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SAG.DU ANŠE.KUR.RA GARin BA₃ut na-ram—d30 ša₂ KUR i-be-lu

(o 13) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a horse's head: omen of Naram-Sin, who ruled over the land.

o 1414

[BE] ⸢U₈⸣ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma ⸢SAG⸣.DU UDU.NITA₂ GAR⸢in⸣ BA₃ut LUGAL—GI.NA ša₂ KUR i-be-lu

(o 14) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a ram's head: omen of Sargon, who ruled over the land.

o 1515

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma ⸢2⸣ SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ DUMU LUGAL AD-šu₂ i-bar

(o 15) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its heads are 2: the king's son will revolt against his father.

o 1616

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma ina UGU a-⸢ru⸣-pi-šu₂ 2 SAG.DU-MEŠ GAR DUMU LUGAL AD-šu₂ i-bar

(o 16) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with 2 heads on top of its neck: the king's son will revolt against his father.

o 1717

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ ⸢U₃⸣.TU-ma 2 SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ MAN ina ḫal-⸢li⸣-šu₂ GARin LUGAL DUMU-MEŠ-šu₂ GAZ-ME

(o 17) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its heads are 2 (and) the second one is located on its upper thigh: as for the king, his sons will kill (him).

o 1818

⸢BE U₈⸣ UR.MAḪ ⸢U₃⸣.[TU]-ma 2 SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ MAN ina ⸢GU₂.MURGU⸣-šu₂ GARin LUGAL DUMU-MEŠ-šu₂ GAZ-ME-šu₂

(o 18) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its heads are 2 (and) the second one is located on its backbone: as for the king, his sons will kill him.

o 1919

[BE] U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.⸢TU⸣-ma 2 SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ MAN ina KUN-šu₂ GAR-ma!(ŠU) <<ina>> ḫal-li-šu₂ i-na!-ṭal GABA.RI

(o 19) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its heads are 2 (and) the second one is located on its tail and faces its upper thighs: correspondence (with the previous apodosis).

o 2020

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ ⸢U₃⸣.TU-ma 2 ( o ) GU₂-( o )šu₂ KUR EN MAN-ma ⸢TUKU?⸣-ma EGIR EN e-mu-qu DUak

(o 20) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its necks are 2: the land will acquire another lord and will follow a strong one.

o 2121

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma 3 SAG.DU-MEŠ!-šu₂ 5 GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ ZIut ELAM.MAki ( o ) KUR ( o ) GU₇

(o 21) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its heads are 3 (and) its feet are 5: an attack of Elam will devastate the land.

o 2222

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma 3 SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ 2 GU₂.MURGU-šu₂ 6 GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ DUMU al-mat-tu₄ AŠ.TE DABbat

(o 22) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its heads are 3, its backbones are 2 (and) its feet are 6: a widow's son will seize the throne.

o 2323

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma 3 SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ NUN til-la-tu₄ ( o ) TUKUši

(o 23) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its heads are 3: the prince will acquire reinforcements.

o 2424

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma 3 SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ 2 GU₂.MURGU-šu₂ 6 GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ u ELLAG₂ 15-šu₂ la₃ GAL₂

(o 24) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its heads are 3, its backbones are 2, its feet are 6 and there is no right kidney: the king's son will seize his father's throne during a rebellion.

o 2525

( o o o o o o o o o ) DUMU LUGAL ina ⸢ḪI⸣.GAR AŠ.TE AD-šu₂ DABbat

o 2626

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma 3 SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ 6 GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ u ELLAG₂ 2.30-šu₂ la₃ GAL₂  ;

(o 26) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its heads are, thier feet are 6 and there is no left kidney: ditto (the king's son will seize his father's throne during a rebellion) and will kill (him).

o 2727

( o o o ) KI.MIN<(DUMU)> LUGAL ina ⸢ḪI⸣.GAR AŠ.TE AD-šu₂ DABbat-ma ( o o o ) GAZ

o 2828

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma 5 SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ 1 SAG.DU UR.MAḪ 1 SAG.⸢DU UR⸣.[... 1] ⸢SAG⸣.DU DARA₃

(o 28) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its heads are 5, 1 lion's head, 1 head of ... [...], 1 wild goat's head, 1 pig's head, 1 wild goat's head, 1 pig's head, 1 male goat's head: there will be chaos in the land and as for the king, his noblemen will [...] him.

o 2929

1 ⸢SAG.DU⸣ ŠAḪ 1 SAG.DU MAŠ₂ SUḪ₃ ina KUR GAL₂-ma LUGAL IDIM-MEŠ-šu₂ [...]-MEŠ-šu₂

o 3030

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma KUN KA₅.A GARin( o ) e-riš-ti ( o ) x [(x)] x ( o ) IM

(o 30) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a fox's tail: request ... [...] ....

o 3131

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma 2 KUN-MEŠ-šu₂ KUR EN MAN-ma TUKU-ma ⸢EGIR⸣ [EN e-mu]-qu DUak

(o 31) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its tails are 2: the land will acquire another lord and it will follow a strong one.

o 3232

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma 2 SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ 2 GU₂.MURGU-šu₂ 2 KUN-MEŠ-šu₂ 6 GIR₃-MEŠ-šu₂ [...] GAL₂ši

(o 32) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its heads are 2, its backbones are 2, its tails are 2, its feet are 6 [...] there will be [...].

o 3333

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma u₃ ma-aš-tam GARin ZIbu ( o o o o ) [...] x ( o ) DU

(o 33) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a bump: attack [...] ... will go.

o 3434

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma <<u₃>> ma-aš-tu₂ ina ZA₃-šu₂ ma-ši-id ZIbu [...] ḪUL

(o 34) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it has a bump on its right: attack [...] evil.

o 3535

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma u₃ ma-aš-tu₂ ina GUB₃-šu₂ ma-ši-id EN x x KA x [x (x)] KUR ( o ) [(x)]

(o 35) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it has a bump on its left: a lord ... [...] a land.

o 3636

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma u₃ ma-aš-tu₂ ina GU₂.TAR-šu₂ ma-ši-id NUN LUGAL ŠU₂tu₂ ( o ) [...]

(o 36) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it has a bump on its back: the prince will [...] the kingship of the world.

o 3737

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma ṣi-ba-ri ( o ) GAR LUGAL UB.DA.LIMMU₂.BA [...]

(o 37) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with ṣibāru-excrescence: the king of the Four Edges [...].

o 3838

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SI-MEŠ GAR-ma 3tu₄ ina 15 GAR LUGAL KUR la₃ UR₅tu₂ ŠU-su ( o ) [KUR]ad₂( o )

(o 38) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with horns and 3 are located on the right: the king (lit. his hand) will conquer a land that is not his.

o 3939

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SI-MEŠ GAR-ma 3tu₄ ina 2.30-šu₂ GAR ZIbu ( o o ) [x] NUN

(o 39) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with horns and 3 are located on the left: attack [...] the prince.

o 4040

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SI-MEŠ GAR-ma!(ŠU) SI 15-šu₂ la-ra-a TUKU ( o o ) NUN GABA.⸢RI⸣ NU TUKUši

(o 40) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with horns and its right horn develops a secondary horn: the prince will not acquire an opponent.

o 4141

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SI-MEŠ GAR-ma!(ŠU) SI 2.30-šu₂ la-ra-a TUKU ZIbu dan-nu

(o 41) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with horns and its left horn develops a secondary horn: massive attack.

o 4242

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SI 15-šu₂ ( o ) la₃ GAL₂ EREN₂ NUN ( o o ) GABA.RI NU TUKU!(GAL₂)ši

(o 42) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its right horn does not exist: the prince's army will acquire no opponent.

o 4343

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SI 2.30-šu₂ ( o ) la₃ GAL₂ EREN₂ KUR₂ ( o o ) GABA!(ZI)( o )RI

(o 43) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its left horn does not exist: the enemy's troop: correspondence (with the previous apodosis).

o 4444

⸢BE⸣ U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SI ina 15 ( o ) GAR NUN KUR KUR₂-šu₂ ( o o ) TIqe₂

(o 44) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a horn on the right: the prince will take his enemy's land.

o 4545

[BE] ⸢U₈⸣ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SI ina 2.30 GAR IRI URI₃ti-ka KUR₂ TIqe₂

(o 45) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a horn on the left: the enemy will take your protected city.

o 4646

⸢BE⸣ U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SI-šu₂ 1-ma ina 15-šu₂ GAR-ma <<u>> la-a-ra TUKU NUN KUR KUR₂-šu₂ u₂-šam-qat

(o 46) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a single horn on its right and it develops a secondary horn: the prince will defeat his enemy's land.

o 4747

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SI-šu₂ 1-ma ina 2.30-⸢šu₂ GAR⸣-ma <<u>> la-a-ra TUKU KUR₂ ina ŠA₃ KUR NAM.RA ( o ) E₃

(o 47) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a single horn on its left and it develops a secondary horn: the enemy will bring out booty from the heart of the land.

o 4848

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SI-MEŠ-šu₂ ša₂ 15 [u] ⸢2⸣.30 la-ra-a TUKU-MEŠ NUN ZIbu ina KUR-šu₂ u₂-šam-qat

(o 48) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its right and left horns develop secondary horns: the prince will overpower an attack in his land.

o 4949

⸢BE⸣ [U₈] ⸢UR⸣.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma 4 SI-MEŠ-šu₂ [ina] 15 u 2.30 GAR-MEŠ NUN kib-ra-a-tu₂ ( o o ) BEel( o )

(o 49) If [a ewe] gives birth to a lion and its four horns are located on the right and on the left: the prince will rule over the (Four) Edges.

o 5050

[BE U₈] ⸢UR.MAḪ⸣ U₃.TU-ma GEŠTU 15-šu₂ la₃ GAL₂ dIŠKUR MAŠ₂.ANŠE ( o o ) RAiṣ

(o 50) [If a ewe] gives birth to a lion and its right ear does not exist: Adad will devastate the livestock.

o 5151

[BE U₈ UR].⸢MAḪ U₃.TU⸣-ma ⸢GEŠTU⸣ 2.30-šu₂ la₃ TUKU dIŠKUR MAŠ₂.ANŠE ša₂ KUR KUR₂ ( o ) RAiṣ

(o 51) [If a ewe] gives birth to a lion and does not have its left ear: Adad will devastate the livestock of the enemy's land.

o 5252

[...] ⸢U₃⸣.TU-ma ⸢GEŠTU⸣-MEŠ-šu₂ ki-la-at-ta-a la₃ GAL₂-MEŠ dIŠKUR MAŠ₂.ANŠE KUR-MEŠ RAiṣ( o )

(o 52) [...] gives birth to [...] and neither of its ears exist: Adad will devastate the livestock of the land.

o 5353

[...] ⸢U₃⸣.TU-ma 1 IGI-šu₂ ina SAG.KI-šu₂ GARat gišTUKUL LUGAL—GI.NA NUN <LUGAL> ŠU₂tu₂ DABbat

(o 53) [...] gives birth to [...] and 1 (single) eye is located on its forehead: weapon of Sargon; the prince will seize the kingship of the world.

o 5454

BE [U₈ UR].⸢MAḪ⸣ U₃.TU-ma it-ti IGI-MIN-šu₂ ka-a-a-ma-na-tu₂ 3tu₄ GAR <<GIR₂>> NUN kib-ra-a-tu₄ ENel

(o 54) If [a ewe] gives birth to a lion and it is provided with two normal eyes (and) a third one: the prince will rule over the (Four) Edges.

o 5555

⸢BE⸣ [U₈ UR].⸢MAḪ⸣ U₃.TU-ma IGI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 la₃ GAL₂ IRI ina BUR₃ši DABbat

(o 55) If [a ewe] gives birth to a lion and its right eye does not exist: the city will be seized through a tunnel.

o 5656

⸢BE⸣ [U₈ UR].⸢MAḪ⸣ U₃.TU-ma IGI-šu₂ ša₂ 2.30 la₃ GAL₂ IRI KUR₂ ina BUR₃ši DABbat

(o 56) If [a ewe] gives birth to a lion and its left eye does not exist: the enemy's city will be seized through a tunnel.

o 5757

[BE U₈] ⸢UR.MAḪ⸣ U₃.TU-ma IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ ki-la-ta-a la₃ GAL₂-MEŠ IRI BI LUGAL ina BUR₃ši DABbat

(o 57) [If a ewe] gives birth to a lion and both its eyes do not exist: the king will seize that city through a tunnel.

Reverse
r 1r 1

BE U₈ ⸢UR⸣.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma IGI-MIN-šu₂ GIM mi-ra-a-nu kat₃-ma KUR in-neš-ši

(r 1) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its eyes are closed like (those of) a puppy: the land will be confused.

r 22

BE U₈ ⸢UR⸣.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SAG.DU GU₄ ( o ) GAR NUN ( o ) KUR ( o ) KUR₂-šu₂ u₂-šam-( o )qat

(r 2) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with an ox's head: the prince will defeat his enemy's land.

r 33

BE U₈ UR.⸢MAḪ⸣ U₃.TU-ma SAG.DU ŠAḪ GAR BA₃ut ( o o ) LUGAL—( o )GI.( o )NA

(r 3) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a pig's head: omen of Sargon.

r 44

BE U₈ UR.⸢MAḪ⸣ U₃.TU-ma SAG.DU DARA₃ ( o ) GAR DUMU LUGAL UGU AD-šu₂ ( o ) DUGUD

(r 4) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a wild goat's head: the king's son will become more important than his father.

r 55

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ ⸢U₃⸣.TU-ma SAG.DU MAŠ.DA₃ GAR TIL BALe UN-MEŠ DUMU-MEŠ-ši-na ana KU₃.BABBAR BUR₂-MEŠ

(r 5) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a wild gazelle's head: end of the reign; the people will dispose of their sons for silver.

r 66

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ ⸢U₃⸣.TU-ma SAG.DU ŠU.LU₂⸢mušen⸣ GAR DUMU al-mat-ti AŠ.TE ( o ) DABbat

(r 6) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a hāzû-bird's head: the son of a widow will seize the throne.

r 77

BE U₈ UR.⸢MAḪ⸣ [U₃.TU]-⸢ma⸣ <SAG.DU> NAM.LU₂.U₁₈.LU GAR LUGAL ŠU-<su> KUR LUGAL!(BI) dan-nu KUR ( o ) DABbat

(r 7) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a human being's head: the king (lit. his hand) will conquer1; a mighty king will seize the land.

r 88

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma IGI UDU ( o ) GAR ta-lit-tu₂ bu-ul NU SI.SA₂

(r 8) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a ram's face: the livestock's offspring will not thrive.

r 99

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma IGI ANŠE GAR su-un-qu dan-nu ina KUR GAL₂ši

(r 9) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a donkey's face: there will be a severe famine in the land.

r 1010

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma IGI UR.GI₇ ( o ) GAR dNERGALₓ(U.GUR) GU₇

(r 10) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a dog's face: Nergal will devour (the land).

r 1111

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma IGI ŠAḪ GAR NIN BA.( o )UŠ₂

(r 11) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a pig's face: a lady will die.

r 1212

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma IGI ḫu-wa-wa GAR NUN GABA.RI NU TUKUši KUR KUR₂-šu₂ GU₇

(r 12) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with the face of Humbaba: the prince will not acquire an opponent; he will devastate his enemy's land.

r 1313

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma KIR₄ SUN₂ ⸢GAR⸣ BAL LUGAL NU SI.SA₂

(r 13) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a wild cow's nose: the king's reign will not thrive.

r 1414

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma KIR₄ <ḫu>-le-e ⸢GAR⸣ su-un-qu ina KUR GAL₂ši

(r 14) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is provided with a shrew's nose: there will be a famine in the land.

r 1515

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma pa-nu-šu₂ ḫi-im-ṣa ar-mu GANBA ( o ) TURer

(r 15) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its face is covered with fatty tissue: business will reduce.

r 1616

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma pa-nu-šu₂ ḫi-im-ṣa ar-mu-ma IGI-MIN-šu₂ pe-ta-a GANBA DU₈ar₂

(r 16) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its face is covered with fatty tissue and its eyes are open: business will be discontinued.

r 1717

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma uzuI₃.UDU ina ap!(GIŠ-)pi-šu₂ GAR KUR NINDA nap-ša₂ ( o o ) GU₇

(r 17) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and fat is located in its nose: the (people of the) land will eat plentiful of food.

r 1818

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma ba-ma-at-su ša₂ 15 uzuI₃.UDU ku-us-sa₃-at ṭu₂!-uḫ₂-di ina KUR GAL₂ši

(r 18) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its right half is covered over with animal fat: there will be abundance in the land.

r 1919

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma ba-ma-at-su ša₂ 2.30 uzuI₃.UDU ku-us-sa₃-at ṭu₂!-uḫ₂-di ina KUR NU [GAL₂]⸢ši⸣

(r 19) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its left half is covered over with animal fat: there will not be abundance in the land.

r 2020

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma uzuI₃.UDU ku-us-su LUGAL GABA.RI NU ⸢TUKUši⸣

(r 20) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and it is covered over with animal fat: the king will not acquire an opponent.

r 2121

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma SAG.DU-su la₃ GAL₂ mu-ut ( o o o o o o ) <NUN>

(r 21) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its head does not exist: death of a prince.

r 2222

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma KA-šu₂ up-pu-uq NIG₂.ḪA.LAM.MA KUR ( o o ) NIN BA.⸢UŠ₂⸣

(r 22) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its mouth is solid: annihilation of the land; a lady will die.

r 2323

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma KA-šu₂ up-pu-uq KUN-su ZAḪ₂at₂ KUR DINGIR-MEŠ-šu₂ TAG₄-MEŠ-šu₂

(r 23) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its mouth is solid, its tail is missing: as for the land, its gods will leave it.

r 2424

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma 2 ( o ) KA-šu₂ INIM KUR UGU ( o ) LUGAL ( o o ) DUGUD

(r 24) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its mouths are 2: the opinion of the land2 will become more important than (that of) the king.

r 2525

BE U₈ UR.MAḪ U₃.TU-ma EME-šu₂ na-ḫi-ra-šu₂ ik-tum KA KUR LUGAL BALat

(r 25) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its tongue covers its nostrils: the opinion of the land will turn against the king.

r 2626

[BE U₈ UR].MAḪ U₃.TU-ma EME-šu₂ IGI-MIN-šu₂ ik-tum NUN ( o o ) KUR-( o )su ( o o o ) BAL-su

(r 26) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its tongue covers its eyes: as for the prince, his land will revolt against him.

r 2727

[BE U₈ UR].MAḪ U₃.TU-ma GABA-su ša₂-at-qa₂-at GALGA KUR BIRaḫ( o )

(r 27) If a ewe gives birth to a lion and its chest is cracked: the mood of the land will be scattered.

r 2828

[BE U₈ UR].⸢MAḪ⸣ U₃.TU-ma ul-la-nu-um-ma ⸢ŠA₃⸣-šu₂ pe-ti KUR SU.GU₇ ( o ) DABbat

(r 28) [If a ewe] gives birth to a lion and its belly is immediately opened: hunger will afflict the land.

r 2929

[...] ⸢U₃⸣.TU-ma ul-la-nu-um-ma ⸢ŠA₃⸣-šu₂ pe-ti-ma ina ŠA₃-šu₂ iz-bu ša₂ ŠAḪ GAR NIN BA.UŠ₂

(r 29) [...] gives birth to [...] and its belly is immediately opened and the anomaly of a pig is located inside it: a lady will die.

r 3030

[...] ⸢U₃⸣.TU-ma ul-la-nu-um-ma ŠA₃-[šu₂] pe-ti-ma ina ŠA₃-šu₂ iz-bu ša₂ UR.GI₇ GAR UMUŠ KUR MANni

(r 30) [...] gives birth to [...] and its belly is immediately opened and the anomaly of a dog is located inside it: decisions for the land will change.

r 3131

[... U₃].TU-ma ul-la-nu-um-ma ŠA₃-šu₂ [pe]-ti-ma ina ŠA₃-šu₂ iz-bu ša₂ ⸢ANŠE⸣ ( o )

(r 31) [...] gives birth to [...] and its belly is immediately opened and the anomaly of a donkey is located inside it: the son of [...] will seize the throne.

r 3232

[...] ( o o o ) GAR ⸢DUMU⸣ [... AŠ].TE DABbat

r 3333

[... U₃.TU]-⸢ma⸣ ul-la-nu-um-ma ŠA₃-šu₂ [...] ⸢ŠA₃-šu₂⸣ iz-bu ša₂ NAM.LU₂.U₁₈.LU GAR  ;

(r 33) [...] gives birth to [...] and its belly immediately [...] and the anomaly of a human being is located inside it: [...] the land will experience famine.

r 3434

[...] ( o o o ) KUR ⸢su⸣-[un-qa] [(...)] ( o ) IGI

r 3535

[... ul]-la-nu-um-ma SIG₂ na-⸢ši⸣ [...] x [(x)] x ( o ) GAL₂ši

(r 35) [...] immediately the hairs are puffed up: there will be [...] ....

r 3636

[...]-MEŠ-šu₂ BA₃ut LUGAL ( o o o) KIŠ

(r 36) [...] its [...]: king of the world's omen.

r 3737

[...]-MEŠ-šu₂ GIR₃ <...> EREN₂-⸢man⸣-[da] NUN KUR KUR₂-šu₂ u₂-šam-qat

(r 37) [...] its [...] foot ... Umman-manda; the prince will defeat his enemy's land.

r 3838

[...] ḫal-la KUR ( o ) ⸢in⸣-( o )neš-( o )ši

(r 38) [...] the upper thigh: the land will become confused.

r 3939

[... mi]-ra?-nu ka-nin!(NIG₂) u [... na]-sik KUR₂ KUR ana KISLAḪ₃ i-kam-mis

(r 39) [...] a puppy, is contracted and [...] is fallen: the enemy will gather the land into a fortress.

r 4040

[...] x KUR₂? [...] x KU E ( o ) GU₄iṭ

(r 40) [...] ... the enemy [...] ... will rise.

r 4141

[...] na-ši ⸢NUN⸣ [...] x SU AŠ.TE DABbat

(r 41) [...] is puffed up: the prince [...]; ... will seize the throne.

r 4242

[...] ( o ) UGU x [...] DUGUDit

(r 42) [...] will become more important than ... [...].

r 4343

[...] ⸢SAG⸣.DU-su UR [...]

(r 43) [...] its head ... [...].

r 4444

[... SAG].DU ANŠE.KUR.RA pa-gar-šu₂ ⸢ANŠE⸣ [...]

(r 44) [...] the head of a horse (and) its body, a donkey [...] the prince will not acquire an opponent.

r 4545

[...] NUN GABA.RI ( o o o ) NU ⸢TUKU⸣

r 4646

[...] NA ŠU ina KUR GAL₂ši KI.MIN ri-ṣi <<KI>> DINGIR ana NUN NUN til-la-a-ti

(r 46) [...] there will be [...] in the land; alternatively, god's help for the prince; the prince, reinforcements.

(catchline)
r 4747

[...] MANe UMUŠmi NIG₂.ḪA.LAM.MA KUR

(r 47) [...] changing of decision; annihilation of the land.

r 4848

[...] ŠUBti₃ NIG₂.UR₂.LIMMU₂.BA

(r 48) [...] epidemic of livestock.

(colophon)
r 4949

[...] ⸢IM 5?⸣ [(x)] BE MUNUS a-rat-ma ša₃ ŠA₃-ša₃ ER₂

(r 49) [...] tablet 5 [...]:"If a woman is pregnant and her foetus keeps crying.

r 5050

[... TIR].⸢AN⸣.NAki [... LIBIR].⸢RA?⸣.BI.GIM AB.SAR BA.AN.E₃

(r 50) [...] Tirana [...]. Written and checked according to its original; junior [...], descendant of Ekur-zakir; hand of Bel-ah-iddin, junior incantation priest.

r 5151

[... BAN₃].DA A mE₂.KUR—[za-kir] ŠU-MIN mU—ŠEŠ—MU lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ BAN₃.DA

1 A word is probably missing here.

2 KA may also be read INIM = awātu here and the expression would be "the affairs of the land".
